title: Automation Commit Guard - diagnosis + guard plan (PLAN/MEMO ONLY)

1_current_repo_state.head: 102202a (Fix deterministic config wiring test path)
1_current_repo_state.factory_tree: clean (no modified/untracked inside trading_research/agentic_factory/ except this new D3 report folder)
1_current_repo_state.head_moved_during_d2_to_d3_gap: Yes: HEAD advanced c02ec13 -> 307d994 (JARVIS Checkpoint Bundle A) between the D2 work and the D3 preflight - a live instance of the race being diagnosed.

2_evidence_found.no_unattended_git_committer: No active hook, no scheduled task, and no daemon performs git add/commit. auto_brain_cycle.bat runs python tools/trading_brain_auto.py --commit, but that tool's commit() only appends to brain_memory text files (append_decision/lesson/next_action/system_change) - it does NOT run git add or git commit. auto_start.bat only launches the dashboard. brain_brief.py / brain_telegram_notify.py do not commit.
2_evidence_found.only_programmatic_git_committer: sparta_commander/research_orchestrator/safe_executor.py (git add/commit at lines ~243/276) is a GATED Commander-lane executor, not the JARVIS step-builder, and is not the source of the interleaved JARVIS step commits.
2_evidence_found.real_source_of_jarvis_commits: A separate INTERACTIVE workstream (another Claude/agent session or operator terminal) that authors docs/jarvis_step_NN_* deliverables and commits them to master. Same git identity ryahai <support@ryah.ai> as the factory commits, which is why the two streams are indistinguishable by author.
interleave_log: 21:29 crypto engine ce499c6 / JARVIS Step 45 b737e4a
2_evidence_found.jarvis_commit_blast_radius: JARVIS commits touch docs/jarvis_*, AND repo-root code: app.py, templates/jarvis.html, tests/test_jarvis_*, tools/jarvis_*. Verified across 307d994, c02ec13, 8133af3, 52cd272 (app.py + tests), 381ad99 (templates/jarvis.html + tests), b737e4a (tools + tests).
2_evidence_found.critical_disjointness_finding: NONE of the inspected JARVIS commits touched trading_research/agentic_factory/. The two workstreams have so far committed to DISJOINT path subtrees. The factory turns touch only trading_research/agentic_factory/; the JARVIS turns touch docs/jarvis_*, app.py, templates/jarvis.html, tools/jarvis_*, tests/test_jarvis_*.

3_risk_model.what_causes_head_races: Two concurrent workstreams committing to the SAME master branch. HEAD advances between factory approval turns because the JARVIS workstream commits in the gap. This is a coordination/branch-topology issue, not a rogue script.
3_risk_model.touches_factory_tree: No - no observed JARVIS commit reached into trading_research/agentic_factory/. Subtrees are disjoint in practice.
3_risk_model.can_stage_unrelated_files: Only if a turn uses `git add .` / `git add <dir>`. The factory turns already avoid this via explicit pathspec, so a factory commit cannot sweep JARVIS files and vice versa. The risk is latent, contained only by manual discipline.
3_risk_model.commits_only_jarvis_or_sometimes_factory: Observed: JARVIS commits are JARVIS-only (docs/jarvis_*, app.py, jarvis templates/tests/tools). They do NOT include factory files. The earlier D9 near-miss was a JARVIS-workstream commit landing a deliverable before the factory turn's approval - a timing/ordering hazard, not a wrong-files sweep.
3_risk_model.can_be_paused_safely: Yes in principle, because the JARVIS committer is an interactive session, not a daemon - 'pausing' means operator coordination (do not run the JARVIS session during a factory commit window). There is no cron to disable.
3_risk_model.branch_workflow_vs_pausing: A dedicated factory-research branch is structurally stronger than pausing: it removes the shared-HEAD coupling entirely, so HEAD ancestry on the research branch is stable regardless of JARVIS activity on master, and a deliberate merge is the only integration point. Pausing relies on human timing every turn; branching relies on topology once.
3_risk_model.residual_risk_level: MEDIUM - contained today purely by explicit-pathspec + per-turn preflight (manual discipline). Not enforced by any guard.

A_pause_jarvis_during_factory_sessions.benefit: Zero code change; immediately available; eliminates interleave during the window.
A_pause_jarvis_during_factory_sessions.risk: Relies on human timing every single turn; easy to forget; does not fix the underlying shared-branch coupling; JARVIS work backs up.
A_pause_jarvis_during_factory_sessions.recommendation: Useful stopgap, not a durable fix.

B_separate_branch_for_factory_research.benefit: Removes shared-HEAD coupling at the topology level: factory branch HEAD is stable regardless of JARVIS commits on master; deliberate merge is the single integration point; preflight ancestry checks become reliable.
B_separate_branch_for_factory_research.risk: Operator must remember to checkout/stay on the branch; merge step adds a small ceremony; if the operator commits factory work to master by habit the benefit is lost.
B_separate_branch_for_factory_research.files_affected: none (git branch only; no source edit)

C_commit_guard_sentinel_file.benefit: A lightweight lock/sentinel (e.g. trading_research/agentic_factory/.commit_lock) that each workstream checks in preflight; whoever holds it commits, the other waits. Cheap; documents intent.
C_commit_guard_sentinel_file.risk: Advisory only unless enforced by a hook (a hook would be a D4/D5 change requiring approval); both sides must honor it; stale lock if a session crashes.
C_commit_guard_sentinel_file.files_affected: a new sentinel/doc file under the factory; OPTIONAL future pre-commit hook (D4/D5, separate approval)
C_commit_guard_sentinel_file.recommendation: Good lighter-weight fallback if branch isolation is rejected; pairs well with B.

D_restrict_automation_pathspec_to_jarvis.benefit: Guarantees the JARVIS committer can never stage factory files, even with git add .
D_restrict_automation_pathspec_to_jarvis.risk: Requires EDITING the JARVIS workstream's commit logic - out of scope for D3 (plan only) and prohibited until D4/D5 with explicit authorization; the JARVIS committer is interactive, so there may be no single script to edit.
D_restrict_automation_pathspec_to_jarvis.files_affected: JARVIS step-builder commit path (location not a single confirmed script; interactive session)
D_restrict_automation_pathspec_to_jarvis.recommendation: Defer; not actionable as a plan-only step.

E_automation_stops_at_staged_state_no_co.benefit: Automation prepares changes but a human performs the commit, centralizing the commit decision.
E_automation_stops_at_staged_state_no_co.risk: Defeats much of the automation's purpose; high friction; still needs someone to remember; does not by itself prevent interleave if the human commits both streams to master.
E_automation_stops_at_staged_state_no_co.files_affected: JARVIS commit path (edit) - gated to D4/D5
E_automation_stops_at_staged_state_no_co.recommendation: Not recommended - too restrictive for the benefit.

F_do_nothing_but_always_explicit_pathspe.benefit: Already in force; zero new work; has prevented every wrong-files sweep so far; cd repo-root && git commit -- <explicit paths>.
F_do_nothing_but_always_explicit_pathspe.risk: Purely manual discipline; one lapse (a git add .) re-opens the sweep risk; does not stop HEAD races, only mis-staging.
F_do_nothing_but_always_explicit_pathspe.recommendation: KEEP as the permanent always-on safety net, but it is necessary-not-sufficient; pair with B.

5_recommended_guard_path.primary: B (dedicated factory-research branch) - the durable structural fix that removes shared-HEAD coupling without editing any JARVIS/automation code.
5_recommended_guard_path.permanent_safety_net: F (explicit-pathspec commits, already standing) - keep on every commit regardless of branch.
5_recommended_guard_path.lighter_fallback_if_B_rejected: C (advisory sentinel/lock file checked in preflight) - optionally hardened into a pre-commit hook later under D4/D5 approval.
5_recommended_guard_path.stopgap: A (pause JARVIS during a factory commit window) - acceptable only until B is adopted.
5_recommended_guard_path.deferred: D and E both require editing the JARVIS/automation commit path and are PROHIBITED in this plan-only step; route to D4/D5 with explicit authorization if ever pursued.
5_recommended_guard_path.rationale: The race is a branch-topology + coordination problem, not a rogue committer. Fixing topology (B) is stronger and lower-blast-radius than editing automation code (D/E). F already prevents file sweeps; B additionally stabilizes HEAD ancestry. No code/scheduler/JARVIS change is required to adopt B+F.

adopt_B_branch_isolation: 1. From a clean tree on master at the intended base, create branch: git switch -c factory-research (or git branch factory-research && git switch factory-research).
2. Do all factory work + decision-record commits on factory-research using the SAME explicit-pathspec method.
3. Preflight each turn checks the factory-research HEAD only; JARVIS commits on master no longer move it.
4. Integrate deliberately: when a milestone is ready, git switch master && git merge --no-ff factory-research (or open a local merge), resolving only factory paths.
5. Never git add .; continue cd C:/SPARTA_BRAIN && git commit -m MSG -- <explicit factory paths>.
keep_F_safety_net: Every commit on any branch uses explicit repo-root-relative pathspecs; never git add . or git add <dir>.
optional_C_sentinel: Define a documented advisory lock convention (e.g. a .commit_lock note) both workstreams check in preflight.
Hardening it into an enforced pre-commit hook is a D4/D5 item requiring explicit approval (hooks change repo behavior).

7_files_likely_to_be_edited_in_d4_d5.B_branch_isolation: No source file edits (git branch/workflow only). Possibly a short doc note in trading_research/agentic_factory/ describing the branch protocol.
7_files_likely_to_be_edited_in_d4_d5.C_sentinel_advisory: A new advisory note/lock file under trading_research/agentic_factory/ (doc only).
7_files_likely_to_be_edited_in_d4_d5.C_or_D_enforced_hook_if_ever_approved: .git/hooks/pre-commit (a real behavior change; explicit approval required; out of scope until D4/D5).
7_files_likely_to_be_edited_in_d4_d5.D_or_E_automation_edit_if_ever_approved: The JARVIS step-builder commit path - location is an interactive session, not a confirmed single script; would require locating and editing that committer; explicit approval required.
7_files_likely_to_be_edited_in_d4_d5.not_to_edit: tools/trading_brain_auto.py is NOT the git committer and must not be edited for this. Do not edit auto_brain_cycle.bat / auto_start.bat for the race (they do not git-commit).

final_line: Repo-Hygiene-D3 is a diagnosis and guard-plan memo only; no automation, scheduler, JARVIS, or trading behavior was changed.
